aiEndpoint.keyKeepPlaceholder: API key — blank keeps the stored key
aiEndpoint.keyDefaultPlaceholder: API key — stored locally in config.toml

compose.offlineDraftTooltip: Home is offline — text is kept on this device until you reconnect
compose.offlineSaveTooltip: Home is offline — reconnect to save
compose.editorPlaceholder: Start writing — the first line becomes the title…

composer.entryPlaceholder: Search notes or ask anything — 'trip notes', 'how do I ease back pain?'

boot.installing: First run: downloading the HomeKB engine (a few MB) …

data.title: Data directory — your data stays on this machine

ai.embeddingTitle: Embedding — turns notes into search vectors (required)
ai.embeddingNote: Switching provider or model changes the vector space — a full reindex (rebuild) is required afterwards.
ai.askTitle: Ask — answers questions over your notes (optional)
ai.askNote: Agents connected over MCP bring their own model; this only powers the built-in Q&A.

index.title: Index — rebuild after changing the embedding model
index.noIndex: No index yet — run compile first
index.drift: Config now uses {{config}}, but the index was built with {{built}}. Rebuild to apply the new model.
index.explain: Embedding vectors are model-specific and can’t be reused — changing the model requires re-embedding every note. Your Markdown files are untouched.
index.costNote_one: Estimated embedding cost: {{cost}} ({{count}} chunk with {{model}}). Summaries are regenerated too, billed at the Summary provider’s rate.
index.costNote_other: Estimated embedding cost: {{cost}} ({{count}} chunks with {{model}}). Summaries are regenerated too, billed at the Summary provider’s rate.

appearance.title: Appearance
appearance.note: Light and dark switch automatically with the OS — there is no manual toggle.

updater.title: App updates
updater.note: Updates download and install in the background; HomeKB switches to the new version the next time it starts.

messages.askUsesSummary: Ask now uses the Summary endpoint
messages.serviceUrlInvalid: A service address must be a public https:// URL
messages.noServiceReachable: No service is reachable right now
messages.connectFailed: Could not connect to the service
messages.restartFailed: Restart failed — quit and reopen HomeKB

drafts.title: Drafts
drafts.description: Kept on your computer and shared across your devices until you save them to your library.
drafts.emptyHint: Anything you write but don't save shows up here.

draft.keptOffline: Home offline — draft kept here until you reconnect

import.emptyFile: Nothing to import — the file is empty

shares.revoked: Share revoked — the link is dead
shares.revokeOffline: Home offline — couldn't revoke the share

rebuild.started: Rebuild started on your home computer — progress on the Status page

settings.askUsesSummary: Ask now uses the Summary endpoint

devices.mintFailed: Failed to generate a pairing code

mdDrop.tooLarge: Too large to import as a note: {{name}}
mdDrop.onlyMarkdown: Only Markdown (.md) files can be imported

net.serviceUnreachable: The connection service is not reachable
net.serviceUnreachableCheckAddress: The connection service is not reachable — check the service address
net.serviceRequestFailed: Request to the connection service failed ({{status}})
net.malformedServiceResponse: Malformed response from the connection service
net.pairCodeInvalid: Pairing code is invalid or has expired

offline.title: Home is offline
offline.subtitle: Your home computer isn't reachable right now. Your notes are safe on it — they just can't be searched from here until it comes back.

steps.title: How to wake it up
steps.wake: Make sure your home computer is awake and connected to the internet.
steps.running: Check that HomeKB (or `homekb tunnel`) is running on it.

help.title: Connection help
help.checkTunnel: On your home computer, run homekb tunnel --status — it should say running. If not, homekb tunnel --install (or turn on “Keep tunnel alive” in the HomeKB app’s Remote tab).
help.reconnects: The tunnel reconnects on its own after sleep or a network change — give it a minute after the computer wakes.
help.repair: Still stuck? Disconnect on the Remote tab and pair again with a fresh code from your home computer.

offline.disconnectAndRepair: Disconnect & pair again with a new code

pair.tagline: Your knowledge base lives on your own computer.
pair.getCodeHint: Get a pairing code from HomeKB on your home computer (the Remote tab), or run homekb pair.
pair.serviceAddressHint: The service your home computer is connected to — shown next to the pairing code in its Remote tab. Scanning the QR fills everything in automatically.
pair.privacyNote: Nothing is stored in between — the service only moves data between you and your computer.
pair.scanInsteadDesktop: On your phone? Scan the QR instead

scanner.cameraDenied: Camera access was denied — enter the code manually instead.
scanner.cameraNotFound: No camera found on this device — enter the code manually instead.
scanner.cameraFailed: The camera could not be started — enter the code manually instead.
scanner.notPairingQr: That QR isn't a HomeKB pairing code — scan the one on your home computer.
scanner.pointCamera: Point your camera at the QR code shown in HomeKB on your home computer.

rebuild.title: Index — rebuild after changing the embedding model
rebuild.noIndexYet: No index yet — run compile first
rebuild.driftWarning: Config now uses {{next}}, but the index was built with {{built}}. Rebuild to apply the new model.
rebuild.bodyWeb: Runs on your home computer; embedding vectors are model-specific and can’t be reused, so changing the model requires re-embedding every note. Your Markdown files are untouched.
rebuild.estimate_one: Estimated embedding cost: {{cost}} ({{count}} chunk with {{model}}). Summaries are regenerated too, billed at the Summary provider’s rate.
rebuild.estimate_other: Estimated embedding cost: {{cost}} ({{count}} chunks with {{model}}). Summaries are regenerated too, billed at the Summary provider’s rate.
rebuild.progressNote: Progress shows on the Status page.

answer.title: Answer
answer.noAnswerNeeded: No AI answer needed — these notes match directly.

noResults.title: No notes matched
noResults.body: Nothing in your library matches “{{query}}” — try different words.
noResults.bodyWithAsk: Nothing in your library matches “{{query}}” — try different words, or ask the AI to answer.

empty.title: Your knowledge base is empty
empty.subtitle: Three ways to add your first notes:

addFiles.title: Add Markdown files
addFiles.desc: Drop <md>.md</md> files into <dir/> on your home computer.

writeHere.title: Write one here
writeHere.desc: Start with the New note tab — the first line becomes the title.

letClaude.title: Let Claude write
letClaude.desc: Connect Claude over MCP and it can save what you learn together.

aiSetup.title: Add your AI keys to get started
aiSetup.subtitle: HomeKB uses AI to make your notes searchable. It needs two keys before it can index anything — both stay on this computer.

embeddingKey.title: Embedding key
embeddingKey.desc: Turns your notes into the search vectors every query runs against.

summaryKey.title: Summary key
summaryKey.desc: Writes each note's summary and category when the index is built.

aiSetup.keysNote: Your keys live in config.toml on this machine. Nothing — keys or notes — ever leaves your computer.

composer.offlinePlaceholder: Home is offline — reconnect to ask
composer.emptyPlaceholder: Add a few notes, then ask anything…

remote.title: Remote
remote.subtitle: Connect your phone or Claude to this home. The connection service only forwards traffic — it never stores your notes.

pairing.title: Pair a new device
pairing.scanHint: scan with your phone, or enter the code on the connect screen
pairing.generateHint: Generate a code, then scan the QR with your phone — or type the code into the HomeKB connect screen or Claude's connector authorization page.

devices.title: Paired devices
devices.empty: Nothing is paired yet — generate a code above and scan it with your phone, or authorize Claude's connector with it.

service.title: Connection service
service.defaultIntro: Generating a code connects this computer to the default HomeKB service so your phone and Claude can reach it — the service only forwards traffic, it never stores your notes. Prefer to host your own or use one shared with you? Pick a different service:
service.emptyList: No services available yet. Add one you host (or someone shared with you), or start this machine's own service below.
service.addressHint: A service address must be publicly reachable over <mono>https://</mono> — it is what your phone connects to.

connection.title: Connection
connection.notHttpsWarning: Not a public <mono>https://</mono> address — fine for testing on this machine's network, but a phone elsewhere can't reach it. Switch to an https service before sharing.
connection.switchWarning: Switching services re-registers this computer — devices paired through the old service will need to pair again.

localService.title: Service on this machine
localService.noThirdParty: Phones connect straight to this computer — no third party
localService.runningHint: The service is running on port 8787. To use it, this machine needs a public <mono>https://</mono> domain pointing at it (reverse proxy or a Cloudflare-style tunnel — your setup). Then add that domain here; auto-select will prefer it.

disconnectCard.hint: Disconnecting removes this device's access token. You can pair again anytime with a fresh code from your home computer — or from any other paired device.

unsupported.title: Scheduled compilation
unsupported.beforeCode: Your home computer’s platform can’t manage a background schedule remotely yet (macOS only for now) — run

schedule.noteAfterCode: on your home computer — indexes new and edited notes on a schedule so search stays fresh. Off = notes still save, but they aren’t searchable until the next compile.

settingsWeb.aiKeyPlaceholder: API key — stored in config.toml on your home computer

homeSection.title: Home computer — your data stays there
homeSection.note: These settings live in config.toml on your home computer. Edits made here are applied there — keys are write-only and are never sent back to this device.

embedding.title: Embedding — turns notes into search vectors (required)
embedding.note: Switching provider or model changes the vector space — run “Rebuild & reindex” below afterwards.

ask.title: Ask — answers questions over your notes (optional)
ask.note: Agents connected over MCP bring their own model; this only powers the built-in Q&A.

appearance.title: Appearance
appearance.note: Light and dark switch automatically with the OS — there is no manual toggle.

share.title: Share this note
share.createFailed: Failed to create the share link
share.anyoneWithLink: Anyone with this link can read the note
share.createdNote: Served live from your home machine — the link works while it's online, and revoking it (Shares tab) kills it instantly.
share.manageHint: Manage or revoke them in the Shares tab.

shares.title: Shared notes
shares.subtitle: Served live from your home machine — nothing is stored anywhere else.
shares.noLink: No link — the home isn't registered with a connection service.
shares.emptyBody: Open a note and tap Share to create a public link — with an optional password and expiry.

shareViewer.missingId: This link is missing its share id.
shareViewer.missingRelay: This link does not say which connection service to ask.
shareViewer.loadFailedStatus: The share could not be loaded ({{status}}).
shareViewer.relayUnreachable: The connection service could not be reached.
shareViewer.protectedBody: Enter the password the author gave you.
shareViewer.expiredBody: The author set an expiry on this share and it has passed.
shareViewer.notFoundBody: This share does not exist or has been revoked by its author.
shareViewer.offlineBody: This note is served live from its author's own computer, which is not connected right now. Try again later.
shareViewer.footer: Served live from the author's own computer

compiling.title: Vectorizing chunks…
compiling.note: Search and answers keep working while it compiles.

dataFlow.title: Data flow
dataFlow.note: Notes compile into a read-only snapshot on your computer — search runs against it locally; nothing is uploaded.

failures.failed_one: {{count}} chunk failed to vectorize — a reindex usually clears this.
failures.failed_other: {{count}} chunks failed to vectorize — a reindex usually clears this.
